scope: D-layer P0: joinery craft (D1) + opening/hardware model (D2). Rules-as-data, same form as ux_rules.json; enforced by audit_a5_joinery.py.

rules.name: top-closure
rules.statement: Any joinery unit taller than 1800mm must reach the ceiling (carcass + top box) — no open dust gap between cabinet top and ceiling. Counter-height units (top <= sill+50) are exempt by design.
rules.provenance: Standard HDB ID carpentry practice (2400 carcass + boxed-up top); project lesson 2026-07-23: user flagged cabinets stopping short of ceiling in renders.

rules.name: equipment-box-up
rules.statement: Wall-mounted equipment (FCU, exposed trunking heads) must be boxed: every open gap between the unit and adjacent soffit/wall/joinery smaller than 200mm is closed with plaster/carpentry box-up, flush with the unit face.
rules.provenance: Standard aircon installation finishing detail; project lesson 2026-07-23: user flagged un-boxed gaps around the above-door FCU.

rules.name: end-closure
rules.statement: Residual gaps between a joinery run end and the nearest wall: <=20mm scribe is fine, 20-300mm must be closed with a filler/blind panel, >300mm must be usable space. Blind-corner panels required where two runs meet in an internal corner.
rules.provenance: Carpentry scribe/filler practice; project lessons: wardrobe east dead pocket (AV gate 2026-07-23), L-closet corner.

rules.name: plinth-and-service-gap
rules.statement: Floor-standing joinery gets a recessed plinth; services (trunking) may run in declared plinth/back service gaps (<=150mm, declared via attach/note). Doors start above the plinth and above any service run crossing the front.
rules.provenance: A2-3 attach rule generalised; installer skirting-run practice.

rules.name: hardware-family
rules.statement: One handle family per room, uniform mounting height band (centre 900-1100mm). Hinged doors: handle at the opening edge (opposite the hinge), so the hardware itself reads as the opening instruction. Sliding doors: recessed edge pulls, no protruding bar. Drawers: centred horizontal bar.
rules.provenance: ID hardware convention; project lesson 2026-07-23: user could not tell how wardrobe doors open (handles were decorative, centred per leaf).
rules.verdict: ID convention, gate-checkable from fronts data

rules.name: door-swing-clearance
rules.statement: Every hinged leaf sweeps a quarter-circle (bounded by span x span x door height). The sweep must be clear of fixed furniture, MEP and walls (soft items exempt: curtains, rug). If clearance in front of the unit is smaller than the leaf width, the design must switch to sliding doors, narrower leaves, or relocate the obstacle.
rules.provenance: A3-4 room-door swing rule generalised to joinery; project lesson 2026-07-23: bay tall-cabinet leaf would hit the nightstand (user: door cannot open).
